name: Jungle Curry
recipeIngredient: 1 lb fresh green beans (if necessary, you may substitute whole frozen ones)
2 tbsp Thai Curry Paste (I like to use the "Key" brand packets of either "Country Curry" or "Red Curry"; other good brands include Mae Ploy and Tommy Tang)
Bamboo shoots (optional; a large can of bamboo tips works well, cut into 1/4 inch thick round slices. Carrots could also be used if you like them)
recipeInstructions:
1 Clean the green bean tips. In a Dutch oven or equivalent vessel, heat the oil.
2 Add the curry paste and fry until fragrant, about 1 minute.
3 Add the chicken broth and bring to a rapid boil.
4 Stir in the green beans and bamboo shoots (or other vegetables). Cook for about 15-20 minutes, adding water if necessary to prevent the liquid from reducing too much.
5 Reduce heat to a hard simmer and continue cooking until the green beans are very tender and have absorbed the flavor of the curry broth.
6 Serve over rice.
